description:
## What
Test follow/post/reply/boost/like/DM against Pleroma and Akkoma. Key known deviation: Pleroma sends `Accept: application/json` on actor GETs (not `application/activity+json`) — the server must serve the AP document for this Accept header too. Also: Pleroma sends `Content-Type: application/activity+json; charset=utf-8`. Fix all incompatibilities. Document green/gap rows in `docs/compat.md`.

## Why
Pleroma and Akkoma have a large installed base. The `Accept: application/json` deviation is a known spec violation that mastodon.social also accommodates — we must match mastodon.social's behavior, not the spec.

## Agent team pattern
- Spawn a Research subagent to: read Akkoma docs on Accept header behavior (https://akkoma.dev/); capture live Pleroma/Akkoma responses for actor fetch and inbox POST shapes; document the exact Accept header values Pleroma/Akkoma sends for each request type.
- Spawn an Implementation subagent to: update the actor GET handler to return the AP document for `Accept: application/json` in addition to `application/activity+json`; ensure Content-Type parsing strips the charset parameter; add any Pleroma-specific activity shape handling.
- Spawn a Test subagent to: add Pleroma fixture files in `tests/fixtures/pleroma/`; write a test that sends a GET to the actor endpoint with `Accept: application/json` and asserts the response is a valid AP document (not HTML); write replay tests for Pleroma inbox shapes.

## Test discipline
- Independent oracle: Akkoma documentation on Accept header behavior; live Pleroma instance responses captured via curl with `Accept: application/json`.
- Test approach: integration test asserting the actor GET handler returns AP JSON for both Accept values; fixture replay for inbox shapes.

## Defensive programming checklist
- No `.unwrap()` in handler paths.
- Actor GET must return AP document for: `application/activity+json`, `application/ld+json; profile="..."`, and `application/json` (Pleroma deviation).
- Content-Type parsing must strip `; charset=utf-8` suffix before matching.
- Must not return HTML error page to AP clients — always return JSON.
- Response Content-Type must always be `application/activity+json` regardless of what Accept was sent.
title: Pleroma/Akkoma compatibility
